subject: enforcing helm and kubernetes version requirements
commit_message:
enforcing helm and kubernetes version requirements

helm 2.8.x
k8s 1.7.14+ and 1.8.9+

versions selected because of:
https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/60814

Issue-ID: OOM-1075

Change-Id: Ife365ac8e8b7812c1fe4fa0764739511c3c6eb55
Signed-off-by: Mandeep Khinda <mandeep.khinda@amdocs.com>

entries.message:
Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Tested a lower helm version install/upgrade.  It worked.  I don't have a K8s 1.7 or something lower than 1.8.8 to test that side of it.

helm version
Client: &version.Version{SemVer:"v2.7.2", GitCommit:"8478fb4fc723885b155c924d1c8c410b7a9444e6", GitTreeState:"clean"}
Server: &version.Version{SemVer:"v2.7.2", GitCommit:"8478fb4fc723885b155c924d1c8c410b7a9444e6", GitTreeState:"clean"}


helm upgrade -i dev local/onap --namespace onap -f onap/resources/environments/disable-allcharts.yaml --dry-run

Release "dev" does not exist. Installing it now.
Error: Chart incompatible with Tiller v2.7.2

Patch Set 1:

I do have k8s version 1.7.7:
[root@kubernetes-master-borislavto-2282-05-01-18 ~]# k version
Client Version: version.Info{Major:"1", Minor:"7", GitVersion:"v1.7.3", GitCommit:"2c2fe6e8278a5db2d15a013987b53968c743f2a1", GitTreeState:"clean", BuildDate:"2017-08-03T07:00:21Z", GoVersion:"go1.8.3", Compiler:"gc", Platform:"linux/amd64"}
Server Version: version.Info{Major:"1", Minor:"7", GitVersion:"v1.7.16", GitCommit:"e8846c1d7e7e632d4bd5ed46160eff3dc4c993c5", GitTreeState:"clean", BuildDate:"2018-04-04T08:47:13Z", GoVersion:"go1.8.3", Compiler:"gc", Platform:"linux/amd64"}
The only problem I have is with aai cassandra statefulSet dns resolution and resources not able to connect to the same cassandra.
Why do we insist on k8s version?

Patch Set 1:

Borislav, I went with K8s 1.8 because that was the version the integration team and documentation are testing on.  

The other reason I selected k8s 1.8 is because as of k8s 1.8 a patch for a CVE made all injected volumes read only and this exposed some problems with some containers that were trying to modify volumes/configmaps injected by k8s.  I see that this was also introduced in 1.7.14 so I will include k8s 1.8 and 1.7 above those versions.

Any issues with requiring helm 2.8?
